title: Split EoTV and EoE Instrumentality the real characters
display_title: Split EoTV and EoE Instrumentality the real characters
wikitext:
[]: [Split] EoTV and EoE Instrumentality: the real characters?             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Jun 01, 2010 2:08 pm Split of Instrumentality: Yay or Nay and Transhumanistic Instrumentality , something of an offshoot/more general version of this Asuka and EoE thread . ______ Legendary wrote: BTW, it should also be noted that in EoTV it is stated several times that "I am the X that exists in your mind." They're not the real version of that person, just the facsimile.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Tue Jun 01, 2010 2:12 pm Who cares about the memory? Fuck memory, I lose that anyways. What I don't lose, what really makes me me is the emptiness I've felt inside that makes me write, the amount of pain I feel at any given moment, how much I wanna fuck that chick sitting two seats away from me. If I want to understand someone, I'll talk to them. I'll never get the whole story, but I'll get enough. Also, I wasn't talking about 26 exclusively. In 25 we get a LOT of "I am the X in your mind", when we're not focusing on one person. Considering that Leliel gives Shinji the exact same spiel, I'm very sorry to tell both you and SSD that we're not supposed to conclude that the Rei/Misato/anyone else Shinji sees is who they really are. MAYBE the last scene of 26. But none of the rest.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Jun 01, 2010 2:14 pm Legend wrote: that we're not supposed to conclude that the Rei/Misato/anyone else Shinji sees is who they really are. MAYBE the last scene of 26. But none of the rest.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Tue Jun 01, 2010 2:20 pm Why not? He knows them well enough. First Rei shows up to him, pointing out what he already knows: she and Kaworu are both people. Then he remembers what Misato tells him, that only people with the will to live deserve to live. Asuka shows up to be contradictory and conflicting, and then Shinji hears Rei tell Asuka that she's the exact same as he is. His father arrives to tell him what's really going on - Instrumentality gives him answers through the man who always knew what was up. Misato questions like she always does, Ritsuko goes science-y like Shinji knows she will. Misato gets angry, but Ritsuko undercuts her with a one liner. At the end of the episode, everyone becomes a chorus for Shinji to hear. They behave exactly as he expects him to and don't tell him anything that he couldn't know except about the nature of Instrumentality itself, which makes sense since he needs to know how it works.

[]:             Quote Post by sephirotic » Thu Jun 03, 2010 1:38 pm Legendary wrote: The metaphor was "perception and the way it shapes the world around him". I then summarized the scene. It works better by assuming they're NOT saying "You can do everything, whee!" (...) The "x in my mind" framework is established as a mutual exchange, and thus not mere imagination... and is also explicitly not used as an intro to any of the scenes I mentioned.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Jun 03, 2010 2:29 pm Legendary wrote: His father arrives to tell him what's really going on - Instrumentality gives him answers through the man who always knew what was up. Misato questions like she always does, Ritsuko goes science-y like Shinji knows she will. Misato gets angry, but Ritsuko undercuts her with a one liner.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Thu Jun 03, 2010 4:25 pm The scene begins with "Once again, Shinji Ikari", actually, and the text that Gendo corrects is addressed to him.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Jun 03, 2010 4:31 pm Legendary wrote: The scene begins with "Once again, Shinji Ikari", actually, and the text that Gendo corrects is addressed to him.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Thu Jun 03, 2010 4:41 pm Shinji says he feels like he's melting away. The text explains it's Instrumentality. Shinji asks where he is later; presumably because the melting has stopped. He's told this is also part of HIP, as far as anyone can tell, but it's also his world. Shinji later (in 26) accuses Misato of being just like him and gets the HIP spiel again because he hasn't caught on. It's that easy.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Jun 03, 2010 4:50 pm Legendary wrote: Why not?

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Thu Jun 03, 2010 4:52 pm In EoTV, we see the sex scene in the case of Misato Katsuragi. She WOULD know it, and she doesn't want the Shinji she sees to see it. Unless it appears twice, once in Shinji's perspective?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Jun 03, 2010 5:03 pm Legendary wrote: In EoTV, we see the sex scene in the case of Misato Katsuragi.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Thu Jun 03, 2010 5:06 pm Because she knows it's the Instrumentality Project and that the image COULD be sent to him, ala EoE. Hell, if you accept Concurrency, it might very well be a delayed reaction to what happened in pre-3I.

[]:             Quote Post by sephirotic » Thu Jun 03, 2010 8:03 pm Legendary wrote: Because she knows it's the Instrumentality Project and that the image COULD be sent to him, ala EoE. Hell, if you accept Concurrency, it might very well be a delayed reaction to what happened in pre-3I.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Thu Jun 03, 2010 8:22 pm The TEXT is the outside stimuli. It forces everyone to confront themselves as Instrumentality draws them in. Once the Congratulations scene is over, Shinji either joins everyone in Instrumentality, losing himself (Separate Endings Theory) or joins Ayanami in the Red Sea (Concurrency Theory).

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Jun 03, 2010 8:44 pm Legendary wrote: Shinji either joins everyone in Instrumentality, losing himself (Separate Endings Theory)

[]:             Quote Post by sephirotic » Thu Jun 03, 2010 8:48 pm Ok, let's make it clear than, Here is the script of the first 4 minutes of episode 26 (TV): NGE_EoTV_26 wrote: 01 Lettering: It was 2016 A.D. 02 Lettering: The thing people lost, 03 Lettering: in other words, the complementation of the mind is unfolding. 04 Lettering: However, there's too little time to describe the entire process. 05 Lettering: Therefore, we will discuss the complementation of the mind of 06 Lettering: one single boy named Ikari Shinji. 07 Lettering: Case 3: In the case of Ikari Shinji. 08 Lettering (Red): Fear 09 Asuka: That I disappear. 10 Shinji: Yet, I can disappear because I feel that I am useless. 11 Rei: Why? 12 Asuka: Because I am useless. 13 Shinji: After all, I am a useless child. 14Shinji: Nobody cares about me. 15 Misato: You run away considering nobody cares about you. 16 Misato: You fear to fail. 17 Misato: You fear to be disliked. 18 Misato: You fear to see the weakness within you. 19 Misato: You too! 20 Misato: We are the same. 21 Ritsuko: Our mind lacks something. 22 Asuka: So we fear it. 23 Rei: So we worry about it. 24 Misato: So we are trying to be one now. 25 Asuka: We are trying to complement by the instrumentality of each other. 26 Rei: That's the instrumentality project. 27 Fuyutsuki: One cannot live without being surrounded by others. 28 Gendou: One cannot live alone. 29 Ritsuko: One is always unique. 30 Kaji: Causing hardship. 31 Asuka: Causing sadness. 32 Misato: Causing the wish to feel anothers' mind and body. 33 Rei: Causing the wish to become one. 34 Fuyu: ,A human is made of weak and fragile materials. 35 Ritsuko: The mind and body are also made of weak and fragile materials. 36 Gendou: So it is necessary to complement by the instrumentality of each 37 Lettering: Why? 38 Gendou (Showing him speaking): There's no other way to live. 39 Lettering (white): Is that true? 40 Rei: Why do you live? 41 Lettering: I've no idea. 42 Asuka: I might live to know why. 43 Rei: For whom do you live? 44 Asuka: Obviously, for me. 45 Shinji: Probably for myself. 46 Lettering: Is that true? 47 Rei:You enjoy your life? 48 Shinji: I wonder. 49 Rei: You enjoy your life? 50 Asuka: Of course yes.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Thu Jun 03, 2010 8:52 pm There's more lettering than that. A lot more of it is in 25, like the "Why did you kill him?" and "What are you afraid of?" rants.

[]:             Quote Post by Rei Makes You Go Splat » Thu Feb 03, 2011 10:52 am Split from another thread into the proper one. ~SSD Bagheera wrote: Ah, EoTV. Missed that bit. Yeah, I can see how that might be seen as a Gendo Wins end.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Feb 03, 2011 11:52 am Rei Makes You Go Splat wrote: I never thought that the characters seen at the end of EoTV were real but rather just impressions in Shinji's mind. He learned that he could change his reality just by his perception, so he imagined Gendo and Yui together in his head.
